headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 10, 2026
article:
**HEADLINE:** "Pirates' Shutout and Diamondbacks' Walk-Off Highlight Thrilling MLB Action"

**Game 1: Pittsburgh Pirates @ Chicago Cubs**

The Pittsburgh Pirates dominated the Chicago Cubs, winning 2-0 behind a strong performance from Shota Imanaga. The right-hander tossed 6.0 innings, striking out 9 batters and allowing 0 earned runs. Bryan Reynolds led the Pirates' offense, hitting a solo home run and driving in 2 runs. Ian Happ was the only Cubs player to reach base multiple times, going 2-for-4.

**Game 2: Arizona Diamondbacks @ Philadelphia Phillies**

The Arizona Diamondbacks edged out the Philadelphia Phillies 5-4 in a thrilling walk-off victory. Brandon Marsh hit a 3-run home run for the Phillies, but it wasn't enough to overcome the Diamondbacks' late-game surge. Bryce Harper went 2-for-4 with an RBI for the Phillies. Michael Soroka started for the Phillies, lasting 5.2 innings and striking out 10 batters.

**Game 3: Miami Marlins @ Detroit Tigers**

The Detroit Tigers shut out the Miami Marlins 2-0 behind a strong outing from Keider Montero. The right-hander tossed 6.0 innings, striking out 7 batters and allowing 0 earned runs. Javier Báez hit a solo home run for the Tigers.

**Game 4: Los Angeles Angels @ Cincinnati Reds**

The Los Angeles Angels routed the Cincinnati Reds 10-2, with multiple players contributing to the offense. Zach Neto hit a solo home run and drove in 2 runs, while Jorge Soler went 2-for-4 with 4 RBIs. Josh Lowe and Elly De La Cruz also hit home runs for the Angels.

**Game 5: Minnesota Twins @ Toronto Blue Jays**

The Toronto Blue Jays exploded for 10 runs, defeating the Minnesota Twins 10-4. Ryan Jeffers hit a 3-run home run for the Twins, while Vladimir Guerrero Jr. went 3-for-5 with an RBI. Brooks Lee and Daulton Varsho also hit home runs for the Blue Jays.

**Game 6: Athletics @ New York Mets**

The Athletics shut out the New York Mets 4-0 behind a strong performance from their pitching staff. Jacob Wilson went 3-for-4 for the Athletics.

**Game 7: New York Yankees @ Tampa Bay Rays**

The Tampa Bay Rays edged out the New York Yankees 5-3, with Yandy Díaz hitting a 2-run home run. Ben Rice also hit a solo home run for the Rays. Steven Matz started for the Yankees, lasting 5.0 innings and striking out 7 batters.

**Game 8: San Francisco Giants @ Baltimore Orioles**

The San Francisco Giants defeated the Baltimore Orioles 6-3, with Willy Adames hitting a solo home run and driving in 2 runs. Jung Hoo Lee also hit a solo home run for the Giants.

**Game 9: Cleveland Guardians @ Atlanta Braves**

The Atlanta Braves routed the Cleveland Guardians 11-5, with Ronald Acuña Jr. hitting a solo home run and driving in 2 runs. Kyle Manzardo also hit a solo home run for the Guardians.

**Game 10: Chicago White Sox @ Kansas City Royals**

The Kansas City Royals shut out the Chicago White Sox 2-0 behind a strong performance from Kris Bubic. The left-hander tossed 7.0 innings, striking out 11 batters and allowing 0 earned runs. Carter Jensen hit a solo home run for the White Sox.

**Game 11: Washington Nationals @ Milwaukee Brewers**

The Washington Nationals defeated the Milwaukee Brewers 7-3, with Jake Bauers hitting a 3-run home run. James Wood went 4-for-5 for the Nationals.

**Game 12: Boston Red Sox @ St. Louis Cardinals**

The St. Louis Cardinals edged out the Boston Red Sox 3-2, with Wilyer Abreu and Jordan Walker both going 2-for-4.

**Game 13: Colorado Rockies @ San Diego Padres**

The San Diego Padres defeated the Colorado Rockies 5-2, with Gavin Sheets hitting 2 home runs and driving in 4 runs. Luis Campusano also hit a solo home run for the Padres.

**Game 14: Houston Astros @ Seattle Mariners**

The Seattle Mariners defeated the Houston Astros 9-6, with Randy Arozarena hitting a 3-run home run. Yordan Alvarez also hit a solo home run for the Astros.

**Game 15: Texas Rangers @ Los Angeles Dodgers**

The Los Angeles Dodgers edged out the Texas Rangers 8-7, with Max Muncy hitting 3 home runs and driving in 3 runs. Andy Pages also hit a 4-run home run for the Dodgers.

**BIGGEST STORY OF THE DAY:**

The Pittsburgh Pirates' 2-0 shutout victory over the Chicago Cubs was the biggest story of the day, with Shota Imanaga dominating on the mound and Bryan Reynolds leading the offense. The Pirates' win marked their first shutout of the season and highlighted their growing strength in the National League Central.
